title: Registry Configuration Engine Schema
description: Schema for Registry Configuration Engine JSON configuration files used with Microsoft Intune

$schema.description: Reference to this JSON schema for validation and editor support

description.description: Description of what this configuration does

created.description: Date the configuration was created (YYYY-MM-DD format)

notes.description: Optional notes or comments about this configuration

settings.description: Array of registry setting groups to apply

settingGroup.description: A group of registry settings to apply to a specific scope and path

name.description: Display name for this setting group (for logging and documentation)

description.description: Description of what this setting group does

scope.description: Target scope for the registry settings. Machine = HKLM, User = all existing user profiles, DefaultUser = template for new users

path.description: Registry path without the hive prefix (e.g., 'SOFTWARE\MyCompany\Settings')

action.description: Action to perform: Set (create/update values), Delete (remove specific values), DeleteKey (remove entire key)

rebootRequired.description: When true, displays a toast notification to the user after remediation indicating a system restart is needed

values.description: Array of registry values to set or delete

registryValue.description: A single registry value to set or delete

data.description: Value data. Type depends on the 'type' field. Supports dynamic variables like {{DATE}}, {{DATETIME}}, {{COMPUTERNAME}}, etc.

skipDetection.description: When true, this value is written during remediation but not checked during detection. Useful for timestamp values like {{DATETIME}} that change on each run.

comparison.description: Comparison operator for detection. Equals (default) = exact match. Numeric operators (GreaterThan, etc.) for DWord/QWord. String operators (Contains, StartsWith, EndsWith) for String. Exists/NotExists check presence. Remediation always sets the data value, except NotExists which deletes.

caseSensitive.description: When true, string-typed comparisons (Equals, NotEquals, Contains, StartsWith, EndsWith) use ordinal case-sensitive matching. Applies to String, ExpandString, and MultiString types. Defaults to ordinal case-insensitive.
